creator: philip@FreeBSD.org
text:
Builds start on a schedule.  If the builder is idle, a new build will be started.  On average, that boils down to one or two builds weekly.  If there is nothing new to build (rarely) the build finishes very quickly.

Note that building and syncing to mirrors are completely separate processes.  Depending on the time it takes to finish a build and "internet weather", some builds are never (or only very briefly) visible on mirrors before being superseded by a newer build.  But this is not visible to end-users.
